definitions: A type of exchange rate regime where a currency's value is fixed against the value of another single currency, a basket of currencies, or a measure of value, such as gold.
An exchange rate policy under which a government commits to keep the exchange rate at a specific level.
examples: Many countries abandoned a fixed exchange rate in favor of floating rates.
Maintaining a fixed exchange rate can require significant intervention.
The economic stability was attributed to the fixed exchange rate.
